reviews.review_title: Best game for what it is
reviews.review_description:
Good combat especially for an online game. Very responsive. No bugs I’ve seen with my good Wi-Fi.
Acting like your character helps and feels immersive. Unique characters. 

Pressure points are useful but you’ll be using them on accident. DEVS

Jumping doesn’t happen too often and people respect 1v1s mostly. Character customization is a little bland but they didn’t have much things you could change anyway. Immersive, and helps when you act like your character. The sound design is underrated because it feels like you’re hitting a meaningful punch. The game has no bugs whenever you have good Wi-Fi.
A good game that keeps you coming is easy to learn but hard to master, like this one.
The cons, however, are that the story is easy to spam through and why are we fighting?
Getting hit where it hurts should damage less. Not hiding anything majorly terrible about this game, just want to make things clear. Overall, it’s a great game for exercise and martial arts in VR when you want it to be. Mostly good player base and minimal bugs. 
8.5/10 I just can’t really play cuz I have bad Wi-Fi
otherwise i could look into this more and rate it higher.
THE PRESSURE POINTS DEVS.

I’m sorry originally I really love this game but two separate times when they updated it I lost all of my profile. 
This included my first profile when I had nearly 100% of the game and beaten every single character. 



Original 5 star review 
It’s not flawless but my goodness does it have plenty of content with a good fun factor in a fighter. 
When they added mixed reality as an option that was huge. The character movement isn’t the most natural and kinda weird to read. 
There’s some issues but pros outweigh cons by a lot.

reviews.review_title: Worlds best fighting game (my fav game)
reviews.review_description:
- Incredibly fun and detailed fighting system

- Outstanding physics

- Worlds best multiplayer combat

- Minigames, special abilities, custom characters

- Constantly active game of full of lobbies

- Consistent and meaningful updates

- Awsome discord community and active dev

- Worlds best weapons combat currently in Beta

reviews.review_title: Good Lord what garbage if you're a real
reviews.review_description:
If you're a real martial artist run far away from this piece of hot garbage for the expense it is. It's just a pain to get to the place where you would fight an opponent. Then their reactions are horrible nowhere near compared to the other VR boxing games. And don't be fooled this is not real Kung Fu at all. For instance I have several different black belts including black sash Shaolin as well as black sash Wing Chun and instructor rating in Jun fan JKD. And then the fact that I was a professional kickboxer gives more credibility to what I'm saying. 
If you have no idea what you're doing you can wander in there and mess around but if you want any sort of real interaction go to thrill of the fight version 1 not the update. Also boxing underdog is pretty good. Stay away the hell away from Creed rise to Glory because it is a pia to actually finally get to a match through all the commercials in the beginning of all the different companies that have contributed to it blah blah blah you just can't get to a real ring match until you're about 10 minutes in. Definitely not worth it. But certainly not as bad as this piece of crap. If you buy it buy sure be sure to not use it right away which was the mistake I made. You can return it if you use it right away and it you find out it sucks like it does.

This game is pretty fun just for movement and I believe a bit of strategy. This is one of the best games on VR for me in my opinion. Looking forward to more updates.

I stumbled across this game, having never heard of it. The graphics are beautiful, from the character shadows to the varied epic background scenery. The combat techniques and the control and movement are simple, intuitive and excellently done. It is one of the most underrated games on the Meta Quest, and one of my favorites as an adult player.
I love playing this game because of the fast paced fighting and the variety of characters and boxes and the character forge but one thing I have to ask can u add more to coop
Meh
With friends that get mad easily , very fun.
Good game

I've owned this game for years on Steam, and I said I'd pay for multiplayer... So I bought the game AGAIN, on the Meta store. The solo dev making this game is sooooo awesome, he constantly puts out new content, including game modes, characters, maps, etc., and mostly importantly, has made the entire game coop! So I'm excited to play it all again in coop.

Btw, let me just explain a few things that make this game amazing: Intricate combat (e.g., different hit spots/hand formations have different effects when you strike opponents), completely varied fighting styles (e.g., each character has a UNIQUE fighting style, not just a single unique special move), extremely customizable gameplay (e.g., adjust/turn off knockback, turn off special "magic" moves to make the game more realistic, increase difficulty, etc.), and the atmosphere (backgrounds/music) is fantastic. I love the arcade mode where you fight waves of attackers, like 5 at a time. It's so awesome. And thankfully, there's no artificial stamina meter like in some other VR fighting games. This makes it a great workout too!

Seriously... buy this game!!!
Super fun combat game, but could have a better story mode with more background info.
